stack: Q6_K GGUF (--no-mtp, no MTP/speculative-decoding head, quantized from the f16 master), llama-server -ngl 999 --parallel 4 --jinja, CTX 20480
llamacpp_build: version: 1 (8f114a9) built with GNU 13.3.0 for Linux aarch64

measured.n_dataset_note: same wp_judge-filtered subset of data/reasoning_dataset/openai_val.jsonl as gate1_f16_baseline_v4.json and ladder_q8.json (n=121) -- like-for-like.

deltanet_state_precision.note: PKG4-02 below-Q8 clause. Q6's DeltaNet ssm_* tensors match the Q6_K tier exactly (120 stay F32, 90 track the tier -- identical split to Q8's 120 F32 / 90 Q8_0). No state tensor dropped below the requested tier.

noise_floor_finding.headline: Q6 (0.8063) scored ABOVE the f16 master (0.8002, the same checkpoint's own uncompressed floor) and ABOVE Q8_0 (0.7851). This ordering is not physically possible as a true quantization effect -- a lossy compression of the f16 weights cannot carry more judge signal than its own source.
noise_floor_finding.conclusion: The +0.61pp Q6 surplus and the -1.51pp Q8 deficit are therefore both consistent with single-seed sampling noise at n=121, not with a real quantization-sensitivity mechanism. The three rungs (f16 0.8002, Q8 0.7851, Q6 0.8063) span 2.12 percentage points against each other while their 95% CI half-widths are each roughly 7-8pp -- the rung-to-rung deltas (1-2pp) are well inside single-run sampling noise, not outside it.
noise_floor_finding.supersedes: ladder_q8.json's headline_finding ("Q8 is NOT lossless on this surgically-pruned MoE" / "the prune made it materially more quantization-sensitive") is FALSIFIED by this rung -- see ladder_q8.json's revised_interpretation block and 27-03-SUMMARY.md. A real degradation mechanism predicts monotonic decay (f16 > Q8 > Q6); the measured ordering is Q6 > f16 > Q8, which a degradation mechanism cannot produce.
